handshape: Claw-5 (all fingers bent, palm facing body)
location: Affected body part (point first, then apply sign at site)
movement: Circular grinding motion ×2–3 reps at the location
non_manual_markers: Furrowed brows, tight lips, head slightly tilted; intensity shown by depth of grimace
training_notes: Collect from ≥15 signers, vary lighting, capture both L and R dominant signers; minimum 50 samples per intensity level (mild/moderate/severe)

location: Forehead — dorsum of hand placed on forehead
movement: Hand placed flat on forehead, then pulled away; repeated twice
non_manual_markers: Eyes slightly glazed, cheeks puffed, slow blink; body sway optional for high fever
training_notes: Temple-variant for turban wearers; bilateral for children; semi-reclined posture for post-partum

handshape: Both: C-hand (curved, thumb & index forming C) pressed to temples
movement: Press inward ×2 then release; optional: single index finger circles at temple
non_manual_markers: Eyes closed or squinting, biting lower lip, head may tilt or bob
training_notes: Single-hand variant for post-stroke elderly; children may exaggerate nodding as NMM

handshape: Open-5 hand (all fingers spread, palm facing body)
movement: Slow circular rub on abdomen ×2–3; for cramps: clenching fist on abdomen
non_manual_markers: Leaning forward, furrowed brow, mouth drawn back
training_notes: Pregnancy variant is flatter/higher; menstruation variant is pelvic-low; children may point to navel

handshape: Open-B hand (flat, palm down, fingers together)
movement: Hand at mouth, forward-downward arc mimicking expulsion; head follows movement
non_manual_markers: Gagging expression, puffed cheeks (nausea), cheeks sucked in (dry heaving), eyes watering
training_notes: Distinguish from KAMZORI by hand direction: forward-arc vs downward drop

movement: Fist bounces at sternum ×2–3 in rhythm with mock cough; head bobs forward each rep
non_manual_markers: Open mouth during each bounce, throat tension visible; dry vs wet cough conveyed by facial intensity
training_notes: Intentional sign marker: deliberate fist placement at sternum; children under 10: mouth-open + body-bob sufficient

hindi_label: Sans lene mein takleef / सांस लेने में तकलीफ
handshape: Both: flat-B hand, palms facing each other at chest level
movement: Hands expand outward then compress inward ×2, mimicking laboured breath; torso heaves
non_manual_markers: Open mouth, rapid shallow breathing, shoulders raised, nostrils flared, eyes wide — distress paramount
training_notes: CRITICAL: output on FIRST qualifying frame, no voting. Elderly: classify on relative bilateral symmetry. Post-stroke: single-hand variant with -0.12 confidence

hindi_label: Seene mein Dard / सीने में दर्द
movement: Fist or claw placed on sternum, slow inward pressing ×2; may drag upward to throat (radiating pain)
non_manual_markers: Grimace, breath-held expression, left arm may be indicated with non-dominant hand (radiation)
training_notes: CRITICAL: output on FIRST qualifying frame. Elderly: escalate at 0.55. Women: lower/left-sided variant. Men: capture SEENE-DARD + arm-drop sequence as CARDIAC_EMERGENCY

movement: Index finger circles at temple ×2–3 (clockwise); optional: whole hand circles in front of face
non_manual_markers: Eyes unfocused or rolling, head slightly swaying side to side, balance adjustment in body
training_notes: Elderly: co-detect KAMZORI within 5s = HIGH RISK COMBINATION (TIA/hypotension). Pregnancy: single-hand variant accepted

location: Sides of body — waist to hip level, arms hanging limp
movement: Both hands drop from waist downward ×2; body slumps shoulders, head droops slightly
non_manual_markers: Drooping eyelids, slack jaw, slow movement, overall body posture is slumped and low-energy
training_notes: Men: low-amplitude subtle variant; Elderly: check against KNOWN-BASELINE; co-detect with CHAKKAR in elderly = HIGH RISK
Reserved: output when model confidence < per-class threshold. Precision must be >= 0.90 to avoid overuse.
Reserved: no valid gesture detected in frame.
